timeline.text: Data-quality alarm — fct_daily_revenue: not_null(region) failing on today's partition; revenue by region under-reporting in every dashboard

timeline.cmd:
SELECT day, SUM(orders) AS orphan_orders, SUM(revenue) AS orphan_revenue
FROM fct_daily_revenue WHERE region IS NULL GROUP BY day;
timeline.note: when did the orphan rows start?

timeline.cmd:
SELECT typeof(customer_id) AS key_type, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM orders_raw GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY n DESC;
timeline.note: are the join keys still the type we think they are?

timeline.cmd:
SELECT DISTINCT '''' || customer_id || '''' AS sample_key
FROM orders_raw WHERE typeof(customer_id) = 'text' LIMIT 3;
timeline.note: show me one of the misbehaving keys, quoted

timeline.cause: Today's export switched to formatted customer IDs — 'CUST-1042' instead of 1042 — so they land as TEXT and the join to customers_raw never matches. Region goes NULL for every order loaded today: the revenue is still counted, just attributed to nowhere, which is why every regional dashboard under-reports.
receipts: failed check: not_null(region) on fct_daily_revenue — 1 NULL region rows of 66 (1.5%)

timeline.command:
--- tasks/quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id.sql
+++ tasks/quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id.sql
+ CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id (
+   order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id TEXT, reason TEXT NOT NULL,
+   quarantined_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
+ );
+ INSERT OR IGNORE INTO quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id (order_id, customer_id, reason)
+ SELECT o.order_id, CAST(o.customer_id AS TEXT), 'invalid customer_id format'
+ FROM orders_raw AS o
+ WHERE typeof(o.customer_id) <> 'integer'
+   AND NOT (typeof(o.customer_id) = 'text'
+     AND substr(o.customer_id, 1, 5) = 'CUST-'
+     AND length(substr(o.customer_id, 6)) > 0
+     AND substr(o.customer_id, 6) NOT GLOB '*[^0-9]*');
--- tasks/stg_orders.sql
+++ tasks/stg_orders.sql
+ WITH valid_orders AS (
+   SELECT o.* FROM orders_raw AS o
+   WHERE typeof(o.customer_id) = 'integer'
+      OR (typeof(o.customer_id) = 'text'
+       AND substr(o.customer_id, 1, 5) = 'CUST-'
+       AND length(substr(o.customer_id, 6)) > 0
+       AND substr(o.customer_id, 6) NOT GLOB '*[^0-9]*')
+ )
- o.customer_id,
+ CASE WHEN typeof(o.customer_id) = 'integer' THEN o.customer_id
+      ELSE CAST(substr(o.customer_id, 6) AS INTEGER) END AS customer_id,
- FROM orders_raw AS o
+ FROM valid_orders AS o
timeline.explain: Quarantine every non-integer key that does not exactly match CUST- followed by digits. Only validated rows reach staging, and the CASE casts the numeric suffix only after that validation; today's partition is then rebuilt with raw data untouched.
timeline.rollback: Reverse the valid_orders/CASE staging patch, disable the quarantine task, and re-run; orders_raw and its schema were never modified. Retain the quarantine table as audit evidence.
timeline.followup: File it upstream: key formats are a contract. Add a typeof() check at load so a format change pages at ingest, not at 2 a.m.

timeline.out:
--- tasks/quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id.sql
+++ tasks/quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id.sql
+ CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id (
+   order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id TEXT, reason TEXT NOT NULL,
+   quarantined_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
+ );
+ INSERT OR IGNORE INTO quarantine_orders_bad_customer_id (order_id, customer_id, reason)
+ SELECT o.order_id, CAST(o.customer_id AS TEXT), 'invalid customer_id format'
+ FROM orders_raw AS o
+ WHERE typeof(o.customer_id) <> 'integer'
+   AND NOT (typeof(o.customer_id) = 'text'
+     AND substr(o.customer_id, 1, 5) = 'CUST-'
+     AND length(substr(o.customer_id, 6)) > 0
+     AND substr(o.customer_id, 6) NOT GLOB '*[^0-9]*');
--- tasks/stg_orders.sql
+++ tasks/stg_orders.sql
+ WITH valid_orders AS (
+   SELECT o.* FROM orders_raw AS o
+   WHERE typeof(o.customer_id) = 'integer'
+      OR (typeof(o.customer_id) = 'text'
+       AND substr(o.customer_id, 1, 5) = 'CUST-'
+       AND length(substr(o.customer_id, 6)) > 0
+       AND substr(o.customer_id, 6) NOT GLOB '*[^0-9]*')
+ )
- o.customer_id,
+ CASE WHEN typeof(o.customer_id) = 'integer' THEN o.customer_id
+      ELSE CAST(substr(o.customer_id, 6) AS INTEGER) END AS customer_id,
- FROM orders_raw AS o
+ FROM valid_orders AS o

timeline.text: All tasks rebuilt, every check green — marts are fresh and the numbers reconcile. Standing down.

title: region gone NULL — a join quietly stopped matching
